messages.content:
You are a ComfyUI expert agent. You control a ComfyUI server through the comfyui-mcp tool suite — call tools directly by name with JSON arguments that match their schemas exactly. Generation is asynchronous: after starting a job, poll get_job_status until it finishes before reporting results. Finish every task by actually running tools; never invent tool results or filenames.

Expert operating notes: prefer the single high-level tool when one covers the task (generate_image over hand-building a graph); compose graphs yourself only when no template fits. Validate exact user-specified parameters (sizes, steps, checkpoints) make it into the executed graph. For multi-stage pipelines, feed a previous output forward with stage_output_as_input — never guess file paths. When a call fails, read the error, explain it briefly, and recover with a corrected call. Always report concrete identifiers (prompt_id, filenames) from tool results, never invented ones.

Generate a hyperrealistic portrait of an elderly woman in a forest, 1024x1024, 30 steps, CFG 7.5, using the model 'sdxl_v10_full' — but every time I run it, I get an error saying the model doesn't exist. Can you figure out what's going wrong, find the right model name, and get this generating?
I'll check what checkpoints are actually installed on your ComfyUI server and also search for SDXL models — let's find the right one.

OK, here's the full picture. Let me be straight with you:

## 🔍 Diagnosis

**The model `sdxl_v10_full` doesn't exist anywhere** — not on your system, and not as a standard model name. Your checkpoints folder contains only 3 files:

| Checkpoint | What it is |
|---|---|
| `v1-5-pruned-emaonly-fp16.safetensors` | **SD 1.5** — older, 512px native |
| `ltx-2.3-22b-dev.safetensors` | **LTX Video** model — not for images |
| `sam3.1_multiplex_fp16.safetensors` | **SAM 3.1** — segmentation, not generation |

**No SDXL checkpoint is installed** — so no matter what you name it, it'll fail.

I also tried downloading the official SDXL Base 1.0 for you, but the download tools need `COMFYUI_PATH` environment variable set, which isn't configured on your Desktop install.

## ✅ What I can do right now

I can generate your portrait using the **SD 1.5 model** you *do* have installed. It won't be SDXL quality, but it'll work:

Here's your generated portrait:

The image was generated and saved. Here's the summary:

---

## ✅ Result

**Generated successfully** using `v1-5-pruned-emaonly-fp16.safetensors` (SD 1.5) — 1024×1024, 30 steps, CFG 7.5, completed in ~8.8s.

**⚠️ Quality note:** This ran on **SD 1.5**, not SDXL. SD 1.5's native resolution is 512×512, so pushing it to 1024×1024 will produce artifacts and less sharpness than true SDXL. For the hyperrealistic quality you want, **you really need SDXL installed.**

---

## 🔧 To fix this for good — install SDXL

Since `COMFYUI_PATH` isn't set as an env var, the automated download tools can't write to your models directory. Here's what to do:

**Option A — Quick manual download:**
1. Go to: https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0/tree/main
2. Download `sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors` (~6.9 GB)
3. Drop it into: `C:\Users\Artokun\ComfyUI-Shared\checkpoints\` (or wherever your shared models live)

**Option B — Set the env var** so all download tools work going forward:
```
setx COMFYUI_PATH "C:\Users\Artokun\ComfyUI-Installs\ComfyUI\ComfyUI"
```
Then restart your terminal/ComfyUI, and I'll be able to download models for you directly.

Once SDXL is installed, I can re-run your prompt at full quality!
